projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The sustained 10-12 device count paired with zero camera and pet motion indicates the household is indoors but stationary, likely engaged in low-mobility weekend activities like watching a movie or resting. While typical for a Saturday, the prolonged inactivity from active Australian Shepherds may warrant a quick wellness check if it's uncharacteristic for them.

The sustained 10-13 device count paired with zero camera or pet motion for four hours on a Saturday afternoon strongly suggests the household is gathered in a single enclosed space or engaged in a prolonged stationary activity, explaining the lack of motion triggers and indicating that occupancy-based automations should remain active rather than triggering vacancy alerts.

The stable 11-13 device count paired with zero camera and pet motion indicates the household is likely hosting a large group gathered in a single camera-blind area for a stationary activity, rather than a network anomaly.

The persistent connection of 11-13 mobile devices combined with zero camera motion or pet activity for four hours strongly indicates a sedentary, contained gathering (like a movie or gaming session) rather than normal daily activity. This matters because it explains the lack of motion alerts while confirming the house is occupied, though prolonged stillness from active dogs warrants a quick wellness check if it continues.

The persistent 11-13 device count paired with zero camera motion or pet activity for four hours on a Saturday morning indicates the presence system is likely counting stationary IoT devices rather than actual people, or the house is unoccupied with devices left connected. This false presence signal may be causing unnecessary automation triggers and should be treated as an unoccupied state until motion confirms occupancy.

The persistent connection of 11-12 devices combined with zero camera motion or pet activity for four hours on a Saturday morning strongly suggests the presence sensors are stale or the household is unoccupied, which could cause automated routines to misfire or leave the home unsecured.

The persistently high device count (11-13) paired with zero camera motion confirms the household is in a prolonged rest state rather than simply leaving electronics on. This matters because Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation suggests she is still recovering, so Steve should anticipate a quiet morning and consider handling household tasks discreetly or postponing non-urgent requests.

The stable high device count combined with zero camera motion suggests electronics are likely left in standby or charging mode overnight, creating ambient light or noise that may be disrupting Roxanne’s sleep cycles rather than indicating active household movement. Verify guest status and consider automating power-down routines for non-essential smart devices to improve rest.

The sustained presence of 12-13 mobile devices during early morning hours, paired with Roxanne’s severely fragmented sleep and elevated heart rate, indicates unusually high indoor activity or restlessness overnight. Since cameras show no movement, the disturbance likely originated from within the home, suggesting a potentially exhausting start to the day that warrants checking on Roxanne’s well-being.

Roxanne’s severely fragmented sleep and elevated heart rate, combined with sustained high device presence and zero camera activity, indicate she is awake but stationary in bed. This matters because I will delay all morning automation and keep the environment quiet until she moves, preventing unnecessary disruption during a likely low-energy start.
